experiment: F-EXP8 resolution test: cross-domain citation rate at N=916
method: generalizer_expert.py cross-domain rate (pre-registered metric: lessons with frontier references spanning 2+ domains). Era analysis by century (L-0..99, L-100..199, etc.). RMT confound check (new domain added S430).

confound_check.verdict: Minimal confound (1 lesson). Rate above target even excluding RMT.

expect: F-EXP8 rate declined to ~5.5% natural ceiling due to DOMEX dilution.
actual: Rate INCREASED to 6.33% (6.22% without RMT confound). S430 decline (5.66%) was a temporary dilution burst. Recent production (L-900-999) sustains 7.0% cross-domain. Era trajectory: 0%->4.6%->2.1%->5.2%->10.3%->10.6%->5.1%->14.1%->7.0%. Mean of last 3 centuries: 8.7%.
diff: Expected continued decline from DOMEX dilution. Actual: recovery above target. DOMEX dilution is transient (burst effect), not structural ceiling. S430 decline was measurement-window artifact.

resolution.evidence: Rate 6.33% exceeds 6% target (n=916). Robust to confound removal (6.22%). Three-century moving average 8.7% > target.
resolution.mechanism: Cross-domain citations emerge organically from meta-analysis, isomorphism tagging, and domain genesis. No dedicated generalizer sessions needed.
resolution.successor: F-EXP11 (body-text integration 0.1% to 2%) is the next-order challenge. Citation-header cross-domain (6.3%) vs body-text integration (0.1%) = 63x gap.
